pass_framing: recreatability-and-cross-doc-consistency cleanup pass surfacing 5 items (P1-P5). P1 closed a `<spec-root>/`-walking scope ambiguity exposed by the intersection of v014 N1 (`minimal` template with `spec_root: "./"`) and v009 I7 (path parameterization): `anchor-reference-resolution` is now scoped to a precisely-defined template-declared set rather than the repo root. P2 clarified who writes `.livespec.jsonc` during seed (wrapper, before post-step doctor-static, using a new required `template` field in `seed_input.schema.json`). P3 added a reserve-suffix canonicalization mechanism so long author slugs don't eat the `-critique` suffix under v015 O3's 64-char truncation. P4 was reshaped during interview from a value-prescription question (what exact `topic` does `out-of-band-edits` write?) into a MUST-level architecture requirement that all `topic` derivations MUST route through the same canonicalization rule, without prescribing implementation mechanism. P5 cleaned up the malformed `6-line shape` vs 7-line example self-contradiction. 3 careful-review passes + 1 dedicated deferred-items-consistency pass caught 16 inconsistencies cumulatively (9 + 2 + 0 + 5).

turns.summary: Determined N=15, NEXT=16, CRITIQUE=15. Found an existing critique draft at proposed_changes/v016-proposed-change-proposal-critique-v15.md from a prior Opus session, verified its line references against the current PROPOSAL.md, confirmed alignment with v015's revision, and began the interview with P1.

P1 interview: explained that under v014 N1 `minimal` with `spec_root: "./"`, the `anchor-reference-resolution` check would walk the project's top-level README, source trees, and .github/ because the existing parenthetical scope was genuinely ambiguous. Recommended P1-A: scope the walk to template-declared spec files + spec-root README when declared + proposed_changes/** + history/**/proposed_changes/** + history/**/<spec-file>.

Locked P1 to A. Moved to P2: explained that PROPOSAL.md's seed wording was silent on whether the wrapper, the LLM via payload, or the LLM via post-wrapper Write tool creates `.livespec.jsonc`; the first option is the only one producing a coherent post-step doctor-static state. Recommended P2-A: wrapper writes `.livespec.jsonc` first, `seed_input.schema.json` gains a required top-level `template` field.

turns.content: A: Wrapper writes it; schema gains `template` (Recommended)

turns.summary: Locked P2 to A. Moved to P3: explained that v015 O3's 64-char truncation on composite `<author>-critique` hints can chop the `-critique` suffix on long author slugs. Recommended P3-A: add a `--reserve-suffix <text>` flag (and Python internal API parameter) that applies truncation to the non-suffix portion only.
Locked P3 to A. Moved to P4: explained the ambiguity of the `topic` YAML field for auto-generated `out-of-band-edit-<UTC-seconds>.md` files and recommended P4-A (verbatim filename stem).

turns.content: The user wants to clarify these questions.
Explain to me why we think option B is bad. I don't know what v14 N6 was talking about.  Having dry code seems better. Why not?

turns.summary: Pulled up v014 N6 (settled on `<topic>-2.md` monotonic collision suffix for propose-change/critique, with `out-of-band-edit-<UTC-seconds>.md` as a SEPARATE always-appended filename convention) and acknowledged that the P4 critique's framing of option B (routing through propose-change's canonicalization) as 'reintroduces a unification N6 rejected' was imprecise. The N6 carve-out was about filename conventions, not the topic-field derivation path. For the `out-of-band-edit-<UTC-seconds>` input, canonicalization is a no-op; the only real tradeoff is coupling out-of-band-edits' topic-field derivation to future changes in propose-change's canonicalization rule vs keeping them decoupled.

turns.content: This is getting down to the implementation level. Are we over specifying? Are we violating the guidelines of the analysis spec that we have lined out? Don't just agree with me. I'm asking you to give me an honest answer.

turns.summary: Gave an honest assessment: yes, P4 was drifting toward over-specification and the hardcode-vs-helper DRY sub-debate was below the spec's abstraction layer. The genuine spec-level question is just 'what value goes in `topic` for auto-generated files?' but the answer is trivially underwhelming (any canonical kebab value satisfies the format; no two implementations materially diverge). The DRY question is pure mechanism. Recommended either demoting P4 to a one-liner or dropping it entirely; P3 is a real architecture issue (user-visible filenames diverge), P4 doesn't reach that bar.

turns.content: Just specify it at the high level. That the canonicalization should be consistent and implemented via the same code path to prevent inconsistency, And whatever else we want to keep consistent about the file name format should be codified as a MUST Requirement, but without specifying the implementation. Does this make sense?

turns.summary: Confirmed the reshaped P4 disposition: add a MUST-level single-canonicalization requirement in §"Proposed-change file format" that applies across all `topic`-derivation paths (user-invoked propose-change, critique's internal delegation, skill-auto-generated artifacts). No mechanism detail (hardcode-vs-helper not prescribed). No deferred-items.md subsection added. Filename-consistency MUSTs from the existing spec (propose-change monotonic suffix; critique `-critique` form; out-of-band-edits `out-of-band-edit-<UTC-seconds>`) are already codified; no additions.

Moved to P5. Recommended P5-A: rewrite the Shebang-wrapper contract as '6 statements (no other statements, no other lines beyond the optional single blank line between the imports and `raise SystemExit`)'. Keep the canonical example as-is. Codify the optional-blank allowance in `check-wrapper-shape`'s algorithm inside the `static-check-semantics` deferred entry.

Locked P5 to A. Wrote `history/v016/proposed_changes/proposal-critique-v15-revision.md` capturing per-item dispositions. Applied all five decisions to `PROPOSAL.md`, `python-skill-script-style-requirements.md`, and `deferred-items.md`: P1 rewrote the `anchor-reference-resolution` section + added a walk-set algorithm subsection to `static-check-semantics`; P2 added wrapper-owned `.livespec.jsonc` write step, widened `seed_input.schema.json` with required `template` field in `wrapper-input-schemas`; P3 added `--reserve-suffix` flag documentation to the topic-canonicalization section + algorithm subsection in `static-check-semantics` with three worked examples; P4 added MUST-level single-canonicalization invariant to §"Proposed-change file format" without mechanism detail; P5 rewrote the shebang contract sentence in both working docs + added `check-wrapper-shape` algorithm subsection.

Careful-review pass 1 caught 9 load-bearing fixes: 5 stale `6-line` phrasings in the style doc; 2 stale `6-line` phrasings in PROPOSAL.md (the argparse-seam rationale at line 317 and the tests/bin/test_wrappers.py tree comment at line 2497); a pre-existing typo (`python-skill-script-script-style-requirements.md`); and an unresolved v015 O2 ripple where `version-directories-complete` still required `README.md` unconditionally despite `minimal` having no per-version README. Also updated the `skill-md-prose-authoring` and `static-check-semantics` prose to reflect that critique now passes author stem + reserve-suffix, not a pre-composed `-critique` topic hint.

Careful-review pass 2 caught 2 load-bearing fixes: the preceding paragraph of §"Proposed-change file format" only mentioned propose-change/critique as `topic` sources, so the MUST-level invariant that followed it read inconsistently; and — during that expansion — the first draft self-violated the P4 disposition by prescribing an exact `topic: out-of-band-edit-<UTC-seconds>` value. Corrected to 'assigns a canonical value of its own choosing' so the architecture-level MUST stands without a mechanism prescription.

Careful-review pass 3 landed 0 load-bearing fixes. End-to-end re-read confirmed all three working docs are self-consistent: `6-line` phrasing is gone except the intentional retrospective quote in the `check-wrapper-shape` algorithm subsection; all v016 P# markers are in place; the reserve-suffix mechanism is consistently described across PROPOSAL.md's propose-change/critique sections and `deferred-items.md`'s `static-check-semantics` + `skill-md-prose-authoring` entries.

Dedicated deferred-items-consistency pass landed 5 load-bearing findings distinct from the careful-review passes: (1) `skill-md-prose-authoring` Source line was stale despite body text widened for P2/P3; (2) the v014 N1 SKILL.md-prose sub-section still said seed's SKILL.md writes `.livespec.jsonc`, stale after P2's wrapper-ownership move; (3) `template-prompt-authoring` Source line didn't record P2's schema-field addition requiring every template's seed prompt to emit `template`; (4) `enforcement-check-scripts` Source line didn't record P5's `check_wrapper_shape.py` algorithm change; (5) two `see PROPOSAL.md §"seed — ..."` cross-references pointed at bolded bullet labels rather than `##`/`###` headings — rewrote as `§"seed" bullet "..."` form. Layout-tree drift check: no new files introduced by v016; no tree edits required. Example-vs-rule alignment: verified reserve-suffix worked-example arithmetic (26+9=35; 73→55+9=64; pre-attached suffix strips and re-appends cleanly).

Finalized v016 history snapshot: copied working PROPOSAL.md to `history/v016/PROPOSAL.md` (verified byte-identical via `diff -q`); moved the critique from `proposed_changes/v016-proposed-change-proposal-critique-v15.md` to `history/v016/proposed_changes/proposal-critique-v15.md`; updated `history/README.md` with the v016 entry (listing distinct pass findings and the cumulative count of 16 inconsistencies across 3 careful-review passes + 1 deferred-items-consistency pass) and updated its final Pointer paragraph to reference v016; wrote `history/v016/conversation.json` with this session's turns.
